name: Golden Rutilated Quartz Sphere
condition: Clean, glassy, dense golden rutile
description: A polished quartz shot through with fine golden rutile needles that catch the light from every angle. Bahia is the classic source, prized for the brightness and density of its rutile. Clean throughout, no repairs.

name: Rutile Stars in Quartz
condition: Well centered star, minor edge wear from polishing
description: Radiating sprays of reddish rutile in the star pattern that made Novo Horizonte famous. Good stars are harder to find than the rough suggests. Well centered with strong contrast.

name: Vanadinite on Matrix
condition: Strong even red, a few small contacts on the back
description: Bright red-orange hexagonal vanadinite with a waxy luster on matrix. Mibladen is the benchmark locality, known for saturated color and crisp crystal edges.

name: Cobaltoan Calcite, Botryoidal
condition: Rich even magenta druze, normal pocket contacts
description: A crust of tiny sparkling calcite crystals in the vivid pink that cobalt gives this material. The Congo is the signature source, and this color is what collectors chase. Photo coming soon.

name: Smoky Purple Fluorite on Quartz
condition: Smoky lavender cubes on sparkling quartz, undamaged face
description: Soft smoky-purple fluorite cubes stepping across a bed of druzy quartz. Inner Mongolia produces this distinctive muted purple. Keep it out of long direct sun so the color holds.

name: Smoky Purple Fluorite, Second Piece
condition: Matched to the first piece, smaller
description: The second of a matched pair of Inner Mongolia smoky-purple fluorites on druzy quartz. Photo coming soon.

name: Gem Green Fluorite
condition: Translucent green, being confirmed before final listing
description: A translucent green piece we have identified as fluorite. We are confirming fluorite versus calcite by hardness and UV before it goes live at full price. Photo coming soon.

name: Epidote on Quartz
condition: Excellent luster, clean terminations, one minor contact
description: Lustrous dark green epidote in sharp bladed sprays against clear quartz. Balochistan is a leading modern source for well terminated epidote with this contrast.

name: Chrysocolla, Botryoidal
condition: Solid teal-blue, being confirmed before final listing
description: Smooth botryoidal chrysocolla in a deep teal-blue. We are confirming natural chrysocolla versus dyed material before it goes live at full price, in keeping with our honest-grading promise.

name: Citrine, Heat-Treated
condition: Fully natural crystal form, color is heat-set
description: A warm amber citrine cluster. This is heat-treated amethyst, the standard and openly disclosed process behind most commercial citrine of this color. We label it as treated, not natural.

name: Pink Halite, Bladed
condition: Bladed pink crystals, keep dry
description: Bladed pink halite from the Searles Lake beds. Halite is water-soluble, so keep it dry and out of humidity. Photo coming soon.

name: Blue Celestite Cluster
condition: Soft blue, glassy crystals
description: A geode section lined with soft blue celestite crystals from Madagascar, the classic source. Blue celestite fades in strong sun, so keep it out of a bright window. Photo coming soon.

name: Botryoidal Malachite
condition: Banded green botryoidal surfaces
description: Rounded botryoidal malachite with the banded green surface collectors look for, from the Congo copper belt. Two pieces available. Photo coming soon.

name: Muscovite with Pyrite
condition: Bladed mica fans with brassy pyrite, solid matrix
description: Olive-gold blades of muscovite mica fanning across a brassy pyrite-bearing matrix. Chosen for the interplay of the mica fans against the metallic pyrite.

name: Pyrite Cluster
condition: Bright metallic cubes
description: A cluster of bright metallic pyrite cubes. Stable in normal display conditions. Photo coming soon.

name: Aragonite "Sputnik"
condition: Full symmetrical spray, minor natural tip wear
description: An interlocking spray of reddish-brown aragonite crystals in the shape collectors call a sputnik. Moroccan aragonite is known for these bold clusters.

name: Green River Fish Plate
condition: Clear fish detail on limestone plate
description: A fossil Knightia fish preserved in a Green River limestone plate, about 50 million years old. Several plates available. Photo coming soon.

name: Favosite Honeycomb Coral
condition: Clear honeycomb structure
description: Fossil honeycomb coral with the classic favosite cell structure. Photo coming soon.

name: Tektites, Natural Impact Glass
condition: Natural sculpted surfaces
description: Natural glass formed by ancient meteorite impacts, with the sculpted surface tektites are known for. Four available. Photo coming soon.

name: Abalone Pearl Shell
condition: High iridescent luster
description: Iridescent abalone shells with strong natural luster. Photo coming soon.

name: Quartz Clusters & Points
condition: Bin material, fun and affordable
description: Common quartz clusters and single points, great starters and gifts. Zone-priced at the booth. Photo coming soon.

name: Amethyst in Matrix
condition: Bin material
description: Amethyst crystals in matrix, affordable decor pieces. Amethyst fades in strong sun. Photo coming soon.

name: Moroccan Geodes
condition: Crack-your-own crystal pockets
description: Small Moroccan geodes with crystal pockets inside. Fun for kids. Photo coming soon.

name: Eocene Shark Teeth
condition: Bagged, real fossil teeth
description: Real fossil shark teeth, roughly 50 million years old, bagged. A great impulse piece. Photo coming soon.

name: "Retro Rocket" Ray-Gun Lamp
provenance: Made by hand in Grimsley, TN
condition: One of one, signed, tested and working
description: A hand-sculpted rocket and ray-gun lava lamp, shaped from Apoxie clay and hand painted, with a blue flow like a jet trail. One of one, signed and working. The kind of piece we build on commission.
